title: shuteria annamica
intro: Shuteria annamica, also known as the Pterocarpus marsupium or Indian red sanders, is a tree species native to India and Southeast Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its roots. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Shuteria annamica has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, the plant's roots are often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, which are used to treat various ailments, and its extracts are also used in herbal preparations for their medicinal properties.
